row086_contract.title: Pose, hand, foot, gait, and contact-phase extraction
row086_contract.completion_criteria: Annotated-clip benchmarks validate visible landmark trajectories and contact phases without fabricated hidden joints.
required_acceptance: partial-body views do not fabricate hidden joints

source_authority.authority: Row086 landmark, trajectory, gait, contact-phase, confidence, benchmark, and hidden-joint requirements
heel-strike inference and ownership-bound hand/body contact architecture
heel-strike event and motion/contact identity preservation boundary
frame and sample error plus visible, clothed, occluded, and multi-actor contact QA

dependency_authority.row086_effect: Frame-level trajectories and phases cannot be certified without an accepted canonical timebase, frame sequence, cuts, and missing-frame policy.
Landmarks, body regions, and contact phases cannot be assigned safely without accepted persistent owner and track identities.

useful_fail_closed_behavior: source image hash and dimension enforcement
pose and depth output count enforcement
row086_non_authority_reasons: The evidence belongs to Row022 and is explicitly motion-reference-only.
It validates body-keypoint counts but does not validate landmark coordinate accuracy against annotations.
Hand and face counts are summarized but are not acceptance gates.
It has no foot-specific taxonomy or accuracy gate.
It does not derive joint trajectories, gait phase, heel strike, sole contact, toe off, hand approach, contact, release, or recoil.
It does not represent observed, hidden, inferred, or unknown joint state.
It explicitly claims no mask, contact, occlusion ownership, target-match, or promotion authority.

adjacent_visual_qa_binding_assessment.review_scope: all 49 pose frames, all 49 depth frames, and seven-frame alignment panel
useful_observations: relative depth spatial and temporal coherence

useful_shape: shot, take, clock, skeleton taxonomy, camera, and coordinate-space binding
instance ownership, visibility, occluders, body-region ownership, and keyframe hashes
missing_or_underconstrained_authority: instances, contacts, and events arrays may all be empty
end_frame is not required to be at or after start_frame
no actual landmark coordinates or confidence arrays
no body, hand, or foot landmark taxonomy requirements beyond an opaque skeleton hash
no trajectory, velocity, acceleration, gait, or contact-phase state
no annotation, metric, threshold, benchmark, or visual-review references

adversarial_shot_pose_schema_probe.interpretation: The closed schema accepts a machine-draft package with reversed frame bounds and no instances, contacts, or events, while rejecting direct fields needed to prove Row086 gait, confidence, partial-view, and hidden-joint semantics.

wave31_pose_audio_force_partial_authorit.correct_result: PASS: 19 required files and 4 scripts checked
wave31_pose_audio_force_partial_authorit.initial_root_mismatch_interpretation: The first invocation omitted --root Plan and was an operator path error, not a product validation failure.
row086_non_authority_reasons: The router consumes a pre-existing force event rather than extracting one from video or landmarks.
The schema is a required-field list rather than JSON Schema validation authority.
The router applies simple force-type routing and does not derive gait/contact phases.
The scorer averages supplied scores and does not compare against annotated clips.

covered_fail_closed_cases: source frame count, order, hash, and dimensions

required_extraction_contract_missing: accepted Row084 canonical timeline and Row085 persistent owner-track inputs
versioned body, hand, and foot landmark taxonomy with left/right and owner identity
observed, inferred, occluded, outside-frame, unknown, and invalid landmark states
per-landmark detector confidence and calibrated uncertainty
coordinate transforms across source, detector, render, depth, and world spaces
temporally filtered trajectories with smoothing delay and residual evidence
joint velocity, acceleration, direction, and confidence
gait state machine with stance, swing, heel strike, sole contact, and toe off
hand approach, contact, compression, recoil, release, and settle state machine
source/target ownership and body/object/surface region binding
partial-view policy that never invents hidden joints
event onset, offset, phase, landmark, and trajectory error metrics
per-event and per-visibility thresholds with certification ceilings
deterministic replay and tamper-resistant extraction receipt
combined landmark, track, contact, and audio visual-review record
acceptance_matrix_required: full-body walking with left and right heel strike, stance, sole contact, toe off, and swing
slow, fast, turning, stopping, starting, and irregular gait
hand approach, contact, compression or recoil, release, and settle
visible hand/foot landmarks benchmark against annotated coordinates
partial-body views mark hidden and outside-frame joints unknown rather than fabricating coordinates
self-occlusion and external occlusion preserve uncertainty and owner identity
multi-actor crossing does not exchange landmarks or contact ownership
camera motion and cuts do not fabricate body acceleration or contact phases
dropped and duplicated frames fail closed through the canonical timeline
low-confidence and anatomically impossible joints are rejected or explicitly unknown
missing hands, feet, or lower body cannot produce certified phases
contact phase requires geometry, depth, motion, persistence, and owner evidence
annotated onset, offset, phase, and trajectory errors stay within registered thresholds
deterministic replay preserves landmarks, phases, uncertainty, and hashes
tampered input, model, annotation, threshold, or receipt is rejected
combined frame, landmark, track, contact, and audio review passes on genuine media

test_and_runtime_boundary.ec2_not_invoked_reason: Both prerequisite rows and the local implementation, semantic contract, annotation, benchmark, and evidence gates already fail; GPU execution would not establish missing authority.

scheduler_and_worker_containment.containment_note: The signed intent was deferred with no routing or wake. Existing paused automations, disabled worker tasks, disabled Mask maintenance tasks, worker snapshots, FLUX2 paths, portfolio paths, and unrelated dirty files were not modified.

hold_decision.reason: Rows084 and 085 remain incomplete. A genuine adjacent Row022 DWPose run provides a useful 49-frame one-person body/hand motion-reference timeline, exact model hashes, ten passing focused tests, and visual review, while the Wave31 force-routing pack validates locally. Neither extracts Row086 gait or contact phases. The shot-pose schema accepts a machine-draft package with reversed frame bounds and empty instances, contacts, and events while rejecting gait, partial-view, confidence, and hidden-joint fields. No direct Row086 production extractor, hand/foot accuracy benchmark, trajectory or phase metrics, partial-view guard, confidence calibration, annotated clips, ownership-bound certification, deterministic replay, current visual hash binding, runtime receipt, or tracker output exists.
hold_decision.safe_next_action: After Rows084 and 085 are accepted, freeze a Row086 landmark and phase contract with body/hand/foot taxonomy, owner binding, observed/hidden/unknown states, calibrated confidence, coordinate transforms, trajectories, gait and contact state machines, annotations, metrics, thresholds, certification ceilings, and deterministic hashes. Reuse the hash-bound Row022 DWPose assets only as an input candidate; implement and test visible, partial, occluded, multi-actor, cut/gap, low-confidence, gait, hand-contact, tamper, and replay cases; then run genuine annotated clips and combined landmark/track/contact/audio review before emitting the tracker-declared evidence. Continue independent Codex PM/QA work meanwhile.
